$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for v4's CharactersRepository.create (Phase-2,
the store-backed capstone sub-unit 3b — the keystone create integration). One
committed file drives BOTH sides:
- the v4 oracle (build-characters-create-fixture.ts + characters-create.ts)
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness characters_create tier-2 test).
WHAT IT PORTS: the full create — _create (slim row, FK nulled) then
ensureCharacterVault (mint a '<name> Character Vault' mount point, scaffold its
preset structure, writeCharacterVaultManagedFields project the managed fields,
then linkCharacterToVault set the FK + confirm it stuck). A character spans TWO
databases, so the differential diffs SIX tables: the MAIN slim `characters` row
+ the MOUNT-INDEX store tables (doc_mount_points / _folders / _files /
_documents / _file_links).
ORPHAN-ON-REWRITE: scaffold writes the default properties.json first;
writeCharacterVaultManagedFields then OVERWRITES it with the managed bag. The
old default-properties file row is left orphaned (writeDatabaseDocument does no
GC), so doc_mount_files / doc_mount_documents carry that extra row — banked here
(same as the groups store-backed differential). The five identity markdown files
+ properties.json are overwritten; physical-description.md / physical-prompts.json
keep their scaffold defaults (this character has no physicalDescription). The one
systemPrompt + one scenario project into Prompts/ and Scenarios/.
REINDEX: the oracle drives the REAL repos.characters.create (no QUILLTAP_JOB_CHILD),
so writeDatabaseDocument's reindexSingleFile runs (deterministic, no model for a
database-backed store); its only persisted divergence from the Rust storage
primitive is the link chunkCount + the doc_mount_chunks rows. The differential
pins chunkCount and excludes doc_mount_chunks.
NORMALIZATION: minted-values remap with ONE shared id-map across all six tables
(characters -> points -> folders -> files -> documents -> links, rows in
natural-key order). Nothing is pinned: the character id + the mount point id +
every file/document/link/folder id are all minted, so the FKs verify by
relationship (characters.characterDocumentMountPointId -> the mount point;
link.fileId -> file.id; etc.). Timestamps -> <ts>; chunkCount -> <cc>.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key (the same one folders/tags/
wardrobe use). NOT the real Friday pepper; safe to commit.

character.name: Evangeline
character.identity: A wandering scholar of forgotten tongues.
character.title: the scholar

systemPrompts.content: You are Evangeline, a scholar of forgotten tongues.
